desc: Madeira is Portugal’s Atlantic island for sea-cliff scenery, levada walks, and spring-to-autumn outdoor days. It works best when timed around your priorities: shoulder months for hiking comfort and value, peak summer for driest weather and ocean time, and winter for lush landscapes with a higher chance of rain and wind.
hls: Levada and mountain trail network with dramatic viewpoints
Funchal old town, market culture, and Madeira wine lodges
Coastal natural pools at Porto Moniz and lava-rock seascapes
Whale and dolphin boat trips in Atlantic waters
Year-round mild temperatures compared with mainland Europe
Strong shoulder-season balance of weather, crowds, and hotel value

todo.name: Funchal old-town + Mercado dos Lavradores
todo.desc: Pair a morning market visit with Rua de Santa Maria lanes, then add a Madeira wine lodge tasting in the afternoon.

todo.name: Monte cable car and tropical gardens
todo.desc: Ride up from central Funchal for city-bay views, then walk the Monte Palace/Botanical Garden area.

todo.name: Levada hike day
todo.desc: Choose a marked levada route suited to fitness and weather; start early for easier parking and cooler trail time.

todo.name: Pico do Arieiro sunrise viewpoint
todo.desc: Go before dawn for above-the-cloud panoramas, but only if wind/visibility forecasts are favorable.

todo.name: Porto Moniz natural pools
todo.desc: Use a full day for northwest-coast drives and lava pools, with flexible timing for sea-state conditions.

todo.name: Câmara de Lobos seafood evening
todo.desc: Finish with grilled fish and poncha in a harbor town atmosphere away from central Funchal hotels.

wifi.note: Funchal hotels/apartments are usually reliable for remote work; uplink and stability can drop in mountain villages.

prac.fltNote: London airports frequently have nonstop options to Funchal (FNC), especially in spring/summer schedules. Shoulder months often price better than July-August and Christmas/New Year peaks.
prac.visa: Portugal is in Schengen; entry conditions depend on passport and stay length, so confirm current rules before travel.
alerts: Mountain weather shifts quickly; cloud, wind, and rain can change trail safety within hours.
Car hire sells out in high season and cruise-ship days can increase Funchal crowding.
Some steep roads and limited parking make early starts useful for popular trailheads.

sweet: April to June and September to October are usually Madeira’s best-value comfort windows, while July-August is driest but busiest.
